docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: sequence()
docs.text: The sequence() method is used to create a sequence of commands that will be executed in order.

docs.title: run ()
docs.text: The run() method is used to execute a command or a sequence of commands when the test case is selected in the driver station. It should return a command that will be executed.

docs.title: waitFor()
docs.text: The waitFor() method is used to create a command that will wait for a certain condition to be met before proceeding to the next command in the sequence. It takes a lambda function as a parameter that defines the condition to be met. For example, waitFor(() -> mechanism.isAtPosition()) will create a command that waits until the mechanism is at a certain position before proceeding to the next command in the sequence.

docs.title: @MARSTEST
docs.text:
Parameters: name

The @MARSTEST annotation is used to define a test case for a MARS module. The name parameter specifies the name of the test case.

docs.title: Extends TestRoutine
docs.text: The TestRoutine class provides the necessary methods to include the test case in the MARS testing framework, which will also publish it in the Elasic chooser and activate it when selected in the driver station.

docs.title: EmptyTest class
docs.text: The class should have the name of the mechanisms that will be tested, followed by "Test". An object of that mechanism (or any other that will be used in the test) should be created to use their commands.

docs.title: Test constructor
docs.text: The constructor should have as parameters the mechanisms that will be used in the tests.

docs.title: Test method
docs.text: Inside of command method, a return statement should be used to return a command that will be executed when the test is selected in the driver station. This command can also be a sequence. See Test commands documentation for more information on how to create commands and sequences.
